steps.title: Locate the angle
steps.content:
The angle $\frac{5\pi}{6}$ is in quadrant II because it is between $\frac{\pi}{2}$ and $\pi$. Its reference angle is
$$\pi-\frac{5\pi}{6}=\frac{\pi}{6}$$

steps.title: Apply the cosine sign
steps.content:
Cosine is negative in quadrant II. The reference-angle cosine is
$$\cos(\frac{\pi}{6})=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
Therefore,
$$\cos(\frac{5\pi}{6})=-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
The answer is A.
